Code fragment: public final TableField<UserRecord, String> EMAIL = createField(DSL.name("Email"), SQLDataType.VARCHAR(90).defaultValue(DSL.field("''", SQLDataType.VARCHAR)), this, "");
Explanation: The EMAIL column uses an empty string as a default value instead of NULL to represent missing data. This makes it harder to distinguish between 'no email provided' and 'intentionally empty email'. Consider using NULL as the default value and making the column nullable.
